subject: Remove unwanted statements for copying jars
commit_message:
Remove unwanted statements for copying jars

The docker image already has the jars related to dcae and ncomp
bundled together. Moreover, the directories /opt/app/dcae*
and /opt/app/ncomp* do not exist on the container. The copy statements
causes misleading errors on docker startup and hence removing them as
they are not relevant.

Change-Id: Ia5364c30a2c88fa109cdae8d8978426f896bc2cc
Signed-off-by: Rashmi Pujar <rashmi.pujar@bell.ca>

entries.message: Patch Set 1: Cherry Picked from branch release-1.0.0.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcae-controller-master-verify-java/28/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcae-controller-master-verify-java/28

Patch Set 1:

Carsten please review as you are most familiar with this code.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

The issue is that the standard setup for using DCAE controller is that JARs related to extensions (e.g., new collectors etc) are placed in /opt/app/dcae-controller-services-XXXX-jars and the code that is proposed removed will copy these JARs into the lib directory of the controller before starting.

It is correct that for the docker setup this is not needed, but that is because the docker setup is only a demo setup with everything copied into the image before it is made. For more real operational setups this is not the case.

I will try to add more documentation to the Guide to explain the more realistic operational setup that AT&T is using.

Abandoned

Thank you for the explanation Carsten, I will abandon this patch then. It would be awesome to get some documentation on the realistic operational setup that is used at AT&T.
